summary: The direct fixer path is constrained, but the claimed closure is incomplete: `runAutomation` still trusts injected `deps.proposePatch` results without re-validating them, so a caller can bypass `validatePatch` and inject privileged step types.

findings.title: Injected fixer implementations bypass `validatePatch` before patch application
findings.recommendation: Wrap all fixer outputs in `validatePatch` at the engine boundary before any `patch.kind` branching or `applyPatch` call. For example, keep `proposePatch` validating its own network result, but additionally do `patch = validatePatch(await fixerFn(...))` in `runAutomation` so injected `deps.proposePatch` implementations cannot return `local_command`/`connector`/`api_call`/`sub_automation` patches directly.
